grant_title: Nita M. Lowey 21st Century Community Learning Center (21st CCLC) grant program
project_area: After-school and summer learning programs providing academic support, enrichment, and family engagement opportunities
demographic_requirements: Programs must serve students in high-poverty areas and prioritize partnerships between schools and community-based organizations
eligibility_summary: Open to Rhode Island LEAs, CBOs, and municipalities partnering to provide high-quality extended learning experiences for students in grades K-12. Programs must align with federal 21st CCLC requirements and Rhode Island After-School Quality Standards.
